Module 2

Let’s talk skincare - Cleansers

Lesson 1 - The pre-cleanse

OBC pre-cleanse

What it looks like:

Product overview

A gentle first cleanse that lifts away make-up, dirt and impurities, including eye make-up.

Prepares the skin for the rest of the skincare routine, helping other products work better while leaving the skin feeling clean, soft and comfortable.

What it does

  • Gently lifts away make-up, dirt and impurities, including eye make-up.
  • Prepares the skin for the rest of the skincare routine, helping other products work better.
  • Leaves the skin feeling clean, soft and comfortable – not tight or dry.

Why it works (from the ingredients)

  • Almond extract helps calm the skin and improve overall skin tone.
  • Grape extract helps support skin moisture and softness.
  • Cruelty-free, with no artificial fragrance or colourants, making it suitable for sensitive skins.

Who is it for

Customers who:

  • Wear make-up daily and want a gentle but effective cleanse.
  • Are starting a proper skincare routine and want to cleanse correctly.
  • Want a luxury but gentle first cleanse without harsh ingredients.
  • Suitable for all skin types, especially dry, sensitive, or combination skin.

How to sell

Start with the need

“Do you wear make-up every day or struggle to fully remove it without drying your skin?”

Position the product

  • Explain that this is the first cleanse – it removes make-up and surface impurities before using a regular cleanser.
  • Highlight that it even removes eye make-up, so there’s no need for multiple products.

Link to routine

  • Recommend using it at night, followed by their OBC cleanser and treatment products.
  • Position it as the foundation step for healthy, balanced skin.

Close the sale

“If you want your skincare to work better and your skin to feel clean but calm, this is the perfect first step.”

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario: OBC Pre‑Cleanse

Set the scene

You’re working in a Beauty Box store when a customer approaches you.

C
“I wear make-up every day, but my skin feels dry and irritated when I remove it. I want something gentle, but I still want my skin to feel really clean.”

You recommend the OBC Pre‑Cleanse and explain how it fits into a proper skincare routine. The customer asks a few questions before deciding.

Knowledge check

The customer asks: “Why do I need a pre‑cleanse?

Can’t I just use my normal cleanser?” What is the best response?

Decision Question 2: The customer says:

“I have sensitive skin and react to fragrance.” How should you position the OBC Pre‑Cleanse?

Decision Question 3: The customer asks:

“Will this help with my skin feeling dry after cleansing?” What is the most accurate answer?

Decision Question 4: You want to close the sale confidently.

Which selling statement is the best fit for this product?

Choose an answer, then click Submit.

Lesson 2 - The hydrating cream cleanser

OBC hydrating cream cleanser

What it looks like:

Product overview

A gentle cream cleanser that cleanses the skin while helping to soothe and refresh.

Its creamy, velvety texture leaves skin feeling nourished, soft and balanced while helping to calm irritation and redness.

What it does

  • Gently cleanses the skin while helping to soothe and refresh.
  • Has a creamy, velvety texture that leaves skin feeling nourished, soft and balanced.
  • Helps calm the skin and reduce irritation and redness.
  • Can also be used to remove eye make-up when used with an eye make-up remover.

Why it works (from the ingredients)

  • Lavender helps calm irritation, reduce redness, and protect the skin from free radicals.
  • Chamomile helps soothe the skin and relieve inflammation.
  • Rosewood and rose geranium support a fresh, comforting cleansing experience.

Who is it for

This cleanser is ideal for customers who:

  • Have dry, sensitive, or easily irritated skin.
  • Feel tightness or discomfort after cleansing.
  • Want a gentle, non-stripping daily cleanser.
  • Prefer skincare that feels comforting and nourishing, not harsh.
  • Suitable for everyday use, especially for customers who prioritise comfort and skin balance.

How to sell

Start with the concern

“Does your skin ever feel dry or tight after you cleanse?”

Position the benefit

  • Explain that this is a gentle cream cleanser.
  • Highlight that it cleans while calming the skin, making it ideal for sensitive or dry skin types.

Link to routine

  • Recommend it as a daily cleanser, especially in the morning or as the second step after a pre-cleanse at night.

Close the sale

“If you want your skin to feel clean, soft and calm after cleansing, this is a great everyday option.”

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario: OBC Hydrating Cream Cleanser

Set the scene

You’re assisting a customer in store who says:

C
“My skin feels dry and tight after cleansing. I want something gentle that still cleans properly, but I don’t want my skin to feel stripped.”

You introduce the OBC Hydrating Cream Cleanser and explain how it works and who it’s best suited for. The customer asks a few questions before deciding.

Knowledge check

Decision Question 1: The customer asks:

“What makes this cleanser different from other cleansers?” What is the best response?

Decision Question 2: The customer says:

“My skin gets red and irritated easily.” Which ingredient benefit should you highlight?

Decision Question 3: The customer asks:

“Will this leave my skin feeling dry after cleansing?” What is the most accurate answer?

Decision Question 4: You want to confidently close the sale.

Which selling statement is the best fit?

Choose an answer, then click Submit.

Lesson 3 - Purifying cleanser

OBC purifying cleanser

What it looks like:

Product overview

A gentle purifying cleanser that uses plant-powered ingredients to cleanse the skin.

Helps purify and soothe the skin while removing excess oil and impurities, without being harsh or stripping.

What it does

  • Gently cleanses the skin using plant-powered ingredients.
  • Helps purify and soothe the skin while removing excess oil and impurities.
  • Uses saponaria, a natural botanical foaming agent, to cleanse without being harsh.
  • Supports the skin’s natural balance with a fruit acid complex.
  • Helps calm itchy, irritated or inflamed skin.
  • Cruelty-free, with no artificial fragrance or colourants.

Why it works (from the ingredients)

  • Tea Tree oil helps address excess oil and calm irritated or inflamed skin.
  • White Thyme has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin and regulate excess sebum.
  • Phyto-extracts of white thyme, tea tree and juniper berry help purify and soothe the skin.

Who is it for

This cleanser is ideal for customers who:

  • Have oily, combination, or blemish-prone skin.
  • Experience excess oil, congestion or breakouts.
  • Want a cleanser that cleans deeply but still feels gentle.
  • Prefer plant-based, fragrance-free skincare.
  • Suitable for customers who want a balancing and purifying daily cleanse.

How to sell

Start with the concern

“Do you struggle with excess oil, breakouts, or skin that feels congested?”

Position the benefit

  • Explain that this is a gentle purifying cleanser, not a harsh stripping one.
  • Highlight Tea Tree for oil control and calming the skin.
  • Reassure customers that it helps balance the skin, not dry it out.

Link to routine

  • Recommend it as a daily cleanser, especially for oily or combination skin.
  • Suggest using it after a pre-cleanse in the evening for a deeper clean.

Close the sale

“If you want your skin to feel clean, fresh and balanced without irritation, this is a great everyday option.”

Test your knowledge

Learning Scenario: OBC Purifying Cleanser

Set the scene

You’re assisting a customer in store who says:

C
“My skin gets oily during the day and I sometimes get breakouts, but I don’t want a cleanser that feels too harsh or drying.”

You recommend the OBC Purifying Cleanser and explain how it helps cleanse and balance the skin. The customer asks a few questions before deciding.

Knowledge check

Decision Question 1: The customer asks:

“What does this cleanser actually do for my skin?” What is the best response?

Decision Question 2: The customer says:

“My skin gets oily and irritated at the same time.” Which ingredient benefit should you highlight?

Decision Question 3: The customer asks:

“Will this cleanser be too harsh for everyday use?” What is the most accurate answer?

Decision Question 4: You want to confidently close the sale.

Which selling statement is the best fit?

Choose an answer, then click Submit.
